francophone.title: EN DIRECT - Canicule : forte hausse du nombre de morts d’animaux domestiques
francophone.excerpt: La société de transports se «prépare à faire face» à l’afflux de voyageurs dès vendredi alors qu’un nouvel épisode de chaleur est attendu sur le pays à partir de ce week-end.

francophone.excerpt: Israël Katz défend un moyen "de protéger" les habitants et communautés de l'Etat hébreu "contre les éléments jihadistes".

francophone.title: « Entroncamento » : en périphérie de Lisbonne, nuit sombre au cordeau
francophone.excerpt: Sublimé par ses plans nocturnes, le polar du Portugais Pedro Cabeleira dépeint un milieu de gangsters misogynes dans une morne cité, où le réalisateur a grandi.

anglophone.title: Canada will take part in Eurovision Song Contest 2027
anglophone.excerpt: The country becomes the first new participant since Australia joined the contest in 2015.

anglophone.title: At least five killed in fire at Antwerp apartment block, say police

anglophone.excerpt: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

anglophone.title: Three die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
anglophone.excerpt: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

anglophone.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
anglophone.excerpt: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.

anglophone.title: 5 safety tips to keep you out of the emergency room this summer
anglophone.excerpt: Experts share guidance on how to prevent common summertime health risks, such as drowning, fire accidents and heat exhaustion.

anglophone.title: A federal law bans late voter roll purges. Republicans are pushing to reinterpret it
anglophone.excerpt: There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.

anglophone.title: Kids with autism are prone to drowning. Florida is trying to prevent that
anglophone.excerpt: Kids with autism can be 160 times more likely than other children to drown. Florida is dedicating state money to a program that prioritizes swim lessons for these kids.

anglophone.title: In Colorado primaries, a democratic socialist wins; Bennet's governor bid ends
anglophone.excerpt: Democrats nominated their candidate to try to unseat a Republican House member and tapped the state's attorney general as a frontrunner for governor.

anglophone.title: How America has celebrated milestone birthdays, from world fairs to the World Cup
anglophone.excerpt: Two founding fathers died on America's 50th birthday, which was marked with toasts and parades. Over time, anniversary celebrations became more extravagant — and more controversial.

anglophone.title: Crowded planes and airports set records, straining the U.S. aviation system
anglophone.excerpt: The summer travel season is setting records again. Airlines are carrying more passengers than ever while operating fewer flights than they did 20 years ago, pushing the limits of the aviation system.

anglophone.title: Months after he sent a harsh email to ICE, agents tracked him to his home and a hotel
anglophone.excerpt: Federal agents went to a Rochester man's home to warn him that an email he sent five months ago could be an illegal threat. He was on vacation but another agent found his hotel hundreds of miles away.
